message:
fix: CI test failures on Windows

- TestVersionChangesCoverage: Add missing 3rd changelog entry for v0.25.1
- TestDefaultPath: Use filepath.Join for cross-platform path handling
- TestDebouncer_CancelWithNoPendingAction: Increase sleep from 60ms to 100ms
  to account for Windows timer imprecision

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

This enables dynamic tips (not just pre-defined ones) to be shown with custom priority and frequency.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:46.645583-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:17:13.733348-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:52:35.096882-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-81a","depends_on_id":"bd-d4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:46.646327-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9e23","title":"Optimize Memory backend GetIssueByExternalRef with index","description":"Currently GetIssueByExternalRef in Memory storage uses O(n) linear search through all issues.

Current code (memory.go:282-308):
for _, issue := range m.issues {
    if issue.ExternalRef != nil && *issue.ExternalRef == externalRef {
        return &issueCopy, nil
    }
}

Proposed optimization:
- Add externalRefToID map[string]string to MemoryStorage
- Maintain it in CreateIssue, UpdateIssue, DeleteIssue
- Achieve O(1) lookup like SQLite's index

Impact: Low (--no-db mode typically has smaller datasets)
Related: bd-1022","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-02T15:32:30.242357-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:17:13.733882-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T11:14:49.172418-08:00"}

This causes CI failures even when all individual tests pass.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T20:58:30.117739-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:58:30.117739-08:00"}

Extract a helper function to DRY this up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-25T19:27:35.075929-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T19:50:21.272961-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T19:50:21.272961-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bgs","title":"Git history fallback doesn't escape regex special chars in IDs","description":"## Problem

In `batchCheckGitHistory`, IDs are directly interpolated into a regex pattern:

```go
patterns = append(patterns, fmt.Sprintf(\`"id":"%s"\`, id))
searchPattern := strings.Join(patterns, "|")
cmd := exec.Command("git", "log", "--all", "-G", searchPattern, ...)
```

If an ID contains regex special characters (e.g., `bd-foo.bar` or `bd-test+1`), the pattern will be malformed or match unintended strings.

## Location
`internal/importer/importer.go:923-926`

## Impact
- False positives: IDs with `.` could match any character
- Regex errors: IDs with `[` or `(` could cause git to fail
- Security: potential for regex injection (low risk since IDs are validated)

## Fix
Escape regex special characters:

```go
import "regexp"

escapedID := regexp.QuoteMeta(id)
patterns = append(patterns, fmt.Sprintf(\`"id":"%s"\`, escapedID))
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:50:30.132232-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:04:06.217695-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:04:06.217695-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bhd","title":"Git history fallback assumes .beads is direct child of repo root","description":"## Problem

`checkGitHistoryForDeletions` assumes the repo structure:

```go
repoRoot := filepath.Dir(beadsDir)  // Assumes .beads is in repo root
jsonlPath := filepath.Join(".beads", "beads.jsonl")
```

But `.beads` could be in a subdirectory (monorepo, nested project), and the actual JSONL filename could be different (configured via `metadata.json`).

## Location
`internal/importer/importer.go:865-869`

## Impact
- Git search will fail silently for repos with non-standard structure
- Monorepo users won't get deletion propagation

## Fix
1. Use `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to find actual repo root
2. Compute relative path from repo root to JSONL
3. Or use `git -C <dir>` to run from beadsDir directly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:51:03.46856-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:05:40.754716-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:05:40.754716-08:00"}
